description: To spit: the seed, the bad water, the taste that warns. Plain expulsion from the mouth — the mouth's own refusal, coined without squeamishness.
sound_symbolism: 'pu' bursts outward from closed lips, 'se' carries it away. Brief, like the act.
grammatical_notes: Transitive or bare: 'mia thinoe puse.' (I spit the seed.) 'shia to puse.' (They spat.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The body knows refusal before the mind does; the verb records its plainest form.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Spitting out the bitter and the spoiled was the palate's veto — a survival reflex with its own etiquette in every culture.
